zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Audley於1310年出生在英國斯塔福爾斯塔福德時，她的父親Hugh Audley 1st Earl of Gloucester20歲，母親Margaret de Clare17歲。她於大約1331年與Sir Ralph Stafford在英國結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和6個女兒的父母。她去世於1348年9月7日在英國肯特托布利其享年38歲，埋葬在英國肯特托布利其。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Audley于1310年出生在英国斯塔福爾斯塔福德时，她的父亲Hugh Audley 1st Earl of Gloucester20岁，母亲Margaret de Clare17岁。她于大约1331年与Sir Ralph Stafford在英国结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和6个女儿的父母。她去世于1348年9月7日在英国肯特托布利其享年38岁，埋葬在英国肯特托布利其。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Audley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Audley in Staffordshire, named from the Old English female personal name Aldgȳth + Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Audley was born in 1310, in Stafford, Staffordshire, England, her father, Hugh Audley 1st Earl of Gloucester, was 20 and her mother, Margaret de Clare, was 17. She married Sir Ralph Stafford about 1331, in England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 6 daughters. She died on 7 September 1348, in Tonbridge, Kent, England, at the age of 38, and was buried in Tonbridge, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Audley est née en 1310, à Stafford, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Hugh Audley 1st Earl of Gloucester, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Margaret de Clare, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Ralph Stafford environ 1331, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 6 filles. Elle est décédée le 7 septembre 1348, à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterrée à Tonbridge, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Audley 1310 in Stafford, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Audley 1st Earl of Gloucester 20 und ihre Mutter Margaret de Clare 17. Sie hat um 1331 in England Sir Ralph Stafford geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Sie starb am 7. September 1348 im Alter von 38 Jahren in Tonbridge, Kent, England und wurde in Tonbridge, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Audley è nata il nel 1310 nel Stafford, Staffordshire, England, sua padre, Hugh Audley 1st Earl of Gloucester, aveva 20 anni e sua madre, Margaret de Clare, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Sir Ralph Stafford circa 1331, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 6 figlie. Morì il 7 settembre 1348, nel Tonbridge, Kent, England, all'età di 38 anni, e fu sepolta a Tonbridge, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Audleyが1310年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードで生まれた時，父Hugh Audley 1st Earl of Gloucesterは20歳，母Margaret de Clareは17歳であった。約1331年にイギリスでSir Ralph Staffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。彼女は1348年9月7日にイギリスケントトンブリッジで38歳で死去し，イギリスケントトンブリッジにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Audley(이)가 1310년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Audley 1st Earl of Gloucester(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret de Clare(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 대략 1331년 영국에서 Sir Ralph Stafford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1348년 9월 7일 영국 켄트 토브리지에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 토브리지에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Audley nasceu em 1310, em Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh Audley 1st Earl of Gloucester, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Margaret de Clare, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Ralph Stafford aproximadamente 1331, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 6 filhas. Ela faleceu em 7 de setembro de 1348, em Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, com 38 anos, e foi sepultada em Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Audley родилась в 1310 г. в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Hugh Audley 1st Earl of Gloucester было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret de Clare, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Ralph Stafford около 1331 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 6 дочерей. Она умерла 7 сентября 1348 г. в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 38 лет и была похоронена в местности тонбридж, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret de Audley nació en 1310, en Stafford, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Hugh Audley 1st Earl of Gloucester, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Margaret de Clare, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Sir Ralph Stafford aproximadamente 1331, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 6 hijas. Murió el 7 de septiembre de 1348, en Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultada en Tonbridge, Kent, Inglaterra.
